Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 2. Understand the Standard P2P Process Before looking at the data I need to recall or define the typical flow of a P2P process Standard Flow 1. Create Purchase Requisition PR - Internal request. 2. Approve Purchase Requisition - Management authorization. 3. Issue Purchase Order PO - Formal order sent to vendor. 4. Receive Goods GR - Physical or digital receipt of items. 5. Vendor Invoice Received IV - Bill from vendor. 6. Match Invoice to PO 3-Way Match - Verify PO GR and IV align. 7. Release Payment - Pay the vendor. 8. Close Case - Finalize the transaction. Key ConstraintsLogic You cant order before you requestapprove. You generally shouldnt pay before receiving goods or matching the invoice risk of paying for nothing. You generally shouldnt receive goods before issuing a PO unless its an emergency blanket order but standard P2P implies PO first. You shouldnt match an invoice before the invoice arrives. Approval usually happens before the PO is issued.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 3. Analyze Each Case Individually Case 1001 PR -> Approve PR -> Issue PO -> Receive Goods -> Invoice Received -> Match -> Pay -> Close. Observation This looks like the textbook definition of a standard P2P process. Verdict Normal. Case 1002 PR -> Issue PO -> Invoice Received -> Release Payment -> Approve PR -> Receive Goods -> Match -> Close. Observations 1. Issue Purchase Order happens before Approve Purchase Requisition. Anomaly 1 2. Release Payment happens before Receive Goods. Anomaly 2 3. Release Payment happens before Match Invoice to PO. Anomaly 3 4. Approve Purchase Requisition happens after Release Payment and Receive Goods. Anomaly 4 Verdict Highly Anomalous. Multiple control failures. Case 1003 PR -> Approve PR -> Issue PO -> Release Payment -> Invoice Received -> Receive Goods -> Match -> Close. Observations 1. Release Payment happens before Receive Goods. Anomaly 1 2. Release Payment happens before Vendor Invoice Received. Anomaly 2 3. Release Payment happens before Match Invoice to PO. Anomaly 3 Verdict Anomalous. Payment made prematurely. Case 1004 PR -> Approve PR -> Receive Goods -> Issue Purchase Order -> Invoice Received -> Match -> Pay -> Close. Observations 1. Receive Goods happens before Issue Purchase Order. Anomaly 1 Verdict Anomalous. Goods received before the legal order was placed.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 4. Synthesize and Structure the Findings Start with a brief introduction acknowledging the standard P2P flow. Go through the anomalous cases one by one 1002 1003 1004. For each case list the specific sequence violations. Explain the business risk associated with each violation eg fraud compliance financial loss. Conclude with a summary of the overall health of the process based on this log.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: 5. Drafting the Content Iterative refinement Refining Case 1002 This is the worst one. Payment before approval Thats a major internal control violation. Payment before goods received Risk of non-delivery. Refining Case 1003 Payment before invoice and goods. This is essentially paying a vendor blindly. High fraud risk. Refining Case 1004 Goods before PO. This is often called a blind receipt. It bypasses the negotiation and pricing control of the PO. Case 1001 Mention it as the baseline for comparison.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 6. Final Review against the Prompt Did I identify anomalies Yes. Did I explain why they are problematic Yes. Did I rely on general P2P knowledge Yes. Is the format clear Yes. 7. Final Polish Ensure the tone is professional and analytical. Self-Correction Make sure to explicitly mention the Three-Way Match concept as its central to P2P controls.
